F0195.label: incoherent
F0195.rationale: The highlighted peak and typical tokens are disparate word fragments, words, names, punctuation, and numbers with no concrete shared semantic, syntactic, or surface-form property evident.

F0366.label: HTML hyperlink attribute syntax
F0366.rationale: Peak firings are almost all the literal HTML hyperlink attribute token `href` (including the adjacent `href=` variant), while typical firings are URL and HTML-link structural fragments such as `http`, domain components, slashes, quotes, and attribute punctuation.

F0371.label: Reuters newswire name
F0371.rationale: Peak firings are all exactly the token “Reuters,” while typical firings are mostly names or fragments of news outlets used in attribution/citation contexts, with a few noisy exceptions.

F0723.label: incoherent
F0723.rationale: The highlighted tokens are highly heterogeneous in both blocks (e.g. “Barrel,” “goal,” “rotor,” “as,” “the,” “with,” and punctuation), with no concrete shared property evident.

F1419.label: incoherent
F1419.rationale: The highlighted tokens are varied common words and punctuation (e.g. “is,” “If,” “can,” “many,” “Everyone,” “At,” “a,” and “.”), with no single concrete property consistently explaining the firings.

F1475.label: gallery “Close” button
F1475.rationale: Peak firings are exactly the UI token “Close” in repeated image-caption/gallery markup, while the typical sample includes several unrelated tokens and is not coherently explained by that form.

F1551.label: incoherent
F1551.rationale: The peak tokens are mostly unrelated, with only repeated fragments around “giveaway” providing a weak local pattern, while the typical tokens have no evident shared property.

F1739.label: incoherent
F1739.rationale: Peak firings are almost all individual digits or punctuation in rule/version-style numeric strings, whereas the typical firings span unrelated words, names, numbers, and abbreviations with no consistent shared property.

F2321.label: incoherent
F2321.rationale: Peak tokens are adjectives or nouns denoting strategic intelligence or skill ("canny," "shrewd," "smart," "wisely," "adroit," and "operator"), but typical tokens are unrelated fragments and words with no consistent shared property.

F2334.label: incoherent
F2334.rationale: Peak firings are mostly disparate proper names such as Alinea, O'Connor, Aronowitz, Fordham, Wix, Leask, Thistle, and Vermont, but include unrelated common nouns like banker and surgery, while typical firings have no consistent shared property.

F2489.label: incoherent
F2489.rationale: The highlighted tokens span unrelated particles, nouns, adverbs, and word fragments (e.g. “in,” “books,” “hooky,” “out,” “casters,” and “were”) with no reliable shared property.

F2627.label: incoherent
F2627.rationale: Peak tokens are mostly pieces or endings of recipe-title food words (e.g. Crumble, Dip, Soup, Bites), but they do not share a consistent token-level property, and typical firings are highly varied.
